topic: Someone receives a package intended for the previous tenant
raw_output:
[story 1]

The delivery notice was addressed to 'Apartment 3B.' I stared at the brown cardboard box sitting on my doorstep, a strange knot tightening in my stomach. It wasn't addressed to me, and the handwriting on the label was unfamiliar, looping and hurried. My breath hitched as I circled the package, my eyes darting to the street, half-expecting someone to materialize from behind a parked car. I cautiously nudged it with the toe of my shoe, the cardboard surprisingly heavy. A tremor ran through my hands as I considered whether I should leave it there or try to figure out who it belonged to.

[story 2]

When the courier handed over the parcel, their smile didn't quite reach their eyes. It was a fleeting moment, but it sent a jolt of unease through me. The box felt cold against my fingertips, the paper strangely slick. I took it inside, placing it on the kitchen counter, and immediately started pacing the living room. Every shadow seemed to deepen, and the silence of the apartment suddenly felt too loud, amplifying the faint sound of my own pulse in my ears. I kept glancing at the door, a restless energy bubbling beneath my skin, wondering what secrets this delivery might hold.

[story 3]

I found the package tucked behind the recycling bins. It was clearly meant for the unit next door, a faded label indicating a previous occupant. My jaw tightened as I picked it up. The contents, visible through the slightly ripped tape, were nondescript but undeniably unsettling—a heavy, wrapped object. I dropped it onto the floor with a muffled thud, my heart beginning to hammer against my ribs. I backed away slowly, my gaze fixed on the hallway, every muscle in my body coiled tight, waiting for something to move or something to happen.
